mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 8.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE spikes >0.5pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when both HOUST and RSAFS rise >1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 98.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when INDPRO declines >1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) and stable FEDFUNDS indicates inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when CPI rises >0.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k concurrent with low unemployment (UNRATE < 7.0%) signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 200k and UNRATE is below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.0% following a contraction signals improved economic outlook, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through enhanced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when GDPC1 grows >2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.0 alongside rising oil prices indicates supply-side shocks that increase corporate costs and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances when INDPRO falls below 99.0 and CPI rises above 233.0.
